title: SIGSEV

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Running 30~ ish nested container on gke with userns=keep-id 

                    "/usr/bin/podman",
                    "run",
                    "--detach",
                    "--userns=keep-id",
                    "--cgroups=enabled",
                    "--umask=022",
                    "--user=1000",
                    "--mount",


### Describe the results you received

Frequent (10% of pods of 1k pods) restarting due to the podman service segfaulting

### Describe the results you expected

containers run.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Running on GKE

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I have the same issue when run one rootless podman-in-podman. One Podman instance start with mounted podman.socket with parameter `--userns=keep-id`. From inside podman instance other instance is started via socket without parameter `--userns=keep-id`.
Podman 5.6.0 on Rocky Linux 9.7.
Frequently around 3%.

Error message:
`Error: Post "http://d/v5.6.0/libpod/containers/create": http: ContentLength=22973 with Body length 0`

It looks that is some problem with reading SubUID/SubGID and that causing crash.
